body_txt: The game of association football is considered by many to be "the world's sport." Nearly every country on Earth loves it. Nearly every. For a long time, one major exception was the United States of America, where the sport has always been overshadowed by baseball , basketball , ice hockey , and their own game, which they call football ( a.k.a. gridiron ), for a variety of reasons. more background It actually had a fair following in America from roughly 1880 to 1935, but it was largely a working class game popular in urban areas and with immigrant communities. But The Great Depression (which led to many clubs folding) and the collapse of the most popular pro league (thanks to a turf war between it and the sport's national governing body) caused a huge decline in its popularity. Many Americans just plain don't like the sport — considering it boring. And this is often represented in the media, where some American characters (especially of the stereotypically manly man variety) will often go out of their way to bash the sport for whatever reason. However, since the 1970s , numerous attempts have been made to make the sport more popular in the States, and it has slowly made some major inroads: America has a fairly successful domestic league ; the FIFA video game series is incredibly popular (although Madden still outsells it in the US); their women's national soccer team has won four FIFA Women's World Cups ; the men's team has vastly improved from joke team to regularly competing with soccer-crazy Mexico for top position in the North America confederation (CONCACAF) with several of its members playing for Europe's top clubs; and many children (more girls than boys) grow up playing it. It probably helps that many chunks of the US have big Hispanic populations, who love the sport. However, in spite of all of that, soccer still lags well behind other sports in America in terms of popularity, and it inspires nowhere near the fervor it does in other countries. The attempts to grow the sport in America have borne some fruit, but have also been greeted by indifference in some circles, and outright resistance in others. For more on the specific reasons behind this trope, see the Analysis page . A specific example of Americans Hate Tingle . Contrast Rugby Is Slaughter , where it's American Football that's seen as the inferior option.
